calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2015 Continued To Feb-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Danielle Zaragoza for (executor) Victoria Dovichi.

The court stated that there has been an objection filed by the administrator's son. Ms. Zaragoza stated that they are close to resolving the issues, requested a 2-4 week continuance, has a letter from the controller and a signed declaration from the son indicating that they are not objecting to a continuance and expects to have an agreement at that time. The petition for final distribution was continued to February 17, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. for further status with the expectation that the parties will have an agreement by then.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Park. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Continued To Jan-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Continued To Dec-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-15-2014 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-04-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Kinyon and Co-counsel Adriano Hrvatin for and with Victoria Dovichi (Petitioner).

Mr. Hrvatin informed the Court that Jesse (the petitioner's son) was not present and that he would be incarcerated for seven months. Mr. Kinyon stated that Jesse had been advised of his rights. The Court stated that it was concerned with what he was doing. Mr. Hrvatin stated that Mr. Shannon had consents to represent Victoria and Jesse to waive any conflict as to representation. The Court stated that it had read the declarations and it was a complete reversal of what the will said, and noted it would have been better if they had separate counsel. Mr. Hrvatin requested additional time if the Court felt they fell short on their representation. Mr. Kinyon presented additional argument to support their position stating that Jesse would be out before all would be settled. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Shannon and Richard Kinyon for Victoria Dovichi.

The Court stated that one of the witnesses to the will was Ezio Paolini who is deceased, but that his signature could be verified by Anne Marie Paolini or Mario Paolini and requested a declaration from either one of them. Mr. Kinyon stated that there was some question as to the validity of the will, not just the authenticity of it and that their position was that they did not support the will. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2008 Continued To Aug-05-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Shannon for and with Victoria Dovichi.

Mr. Shannon stated that the Estate no longer existed as it did 20 years ago, that the personal property was gone, that the assets were depleted and that there were liens on the property. In addition, he informed the Court that the other Beneficiary was incarcerated and that the Beneficiary was consenting to the petition but that he did not have it in writing. He also stated that the family was still living in the house and they wanted to remain living there. The Court requested a written consent from the other Beneficiary. The matter was continued to August 5, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Peppina Thompson CSR#7433. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.
